title: Pfeifferago obcordata
taxonomy: Pfeifferago obcordata is classified within the family Ericaceae, which includes other heath and heather species. This classification places Pfeifferago obcordata in the order Ericales, a group of flowering plants known for their typically showy flowers and often acidic soil preferences. The family Ericaceae is further divided into several subfamilies, with Pfeifferago obcordata being a part of the subfamily Epigaeoideae. At the rank of genus, Pfeifferago is a monotypic genus, meaning it contains only one species. Further classification at the rank of species is also recognized for Pfeifferago obcordata.

intro: Pfeifferago obcordata, also known as the pfeifferago plant, is a flowering plant species in the family Ericaceae. The plant is valued for its potential health benefits, including its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antioxidant properties, which may aid in the treatment of various ailments such as wounds, infections, and oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Pfeifferago obcordata include phenolic acids, flavonoids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. The plant is typically used to prepare herbal teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used in traditional medicine to support wound healing and immune system function.
